title: Parole rate is up in Mass., including for people with life sentences
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Massachusetts parole rate increased by 3.5% in 2024 due to sentencing decisions like Commonwealth v. Sheldon Mattis.
Parole rate for life sentences in Massachusetts rose from 57% in 2023 to 72% in 2024 following key legal changes.
In 2024, 210 individuals affected by the Mattis decision were identified for parole eligibility, with 144 immediately eligible.

title: New photo released in unsolved 1997 homicide of a N.H. woman
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: New Hampshire officials release a photo of Rosalie Miller in hopes of finding new witnesses for her 1997 unsolved homicide.
Rosalie Miller was last seen in Manchester, NH, in December 1996; her body was found a month later in Auburn.
The cause of death was ligature strangulation and authorities seek information from anyone who saw or spoke with her in late 1996.

title: Man allegedly kills man in R.I. before causing car crash that killed 2 in Mass.
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Demitri Sousa, 28, allegedly shot and killed Javon Lawson in Cranston, Rhode Island, due to a dispute over a female acquaintance.
Sousa allegedly fled the scene in a white Infiniti, later seen in Massachusetts, and caused a fatal crash in Swansea.
The Swansea crash resulted in the deaths of a man and a woman; police found a pistol and ammunition in Sousa's car.

title: Weighing the pros and cons of a Robert Thomas trade for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney indicates interest in trading for St. Louis center Robert Thomas before the NHL trade deadline.
Robert Thomas, 26, has exceeded 80 points in each of the last two NHL seasons, seen as a long-term asset.
St. Louis might delay trading Thomas, despite Bruins' potential offer, seeking more assets or better timing.

title: An update: Did a Brooklyn couple kill a neighbor’s trees for a better view in Maine?
author: New York Times Service
bullets: A New York couple was fined $3,000 for allegedly poisoning trees in Rockport, Maine, without admitting guilt.
The Maine Board of Pesticides Control settled with Stephen Antonson and Kathleen Hackett five years after the trees' deaths.
Ruth Graham's family suggested a donation and public apology, but the couple's lawyer did not address these proposals.

title: 10 combine standouts that could make sense for Patriots to select in April’s NFL Draft
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Texas A&M's KC Concepcion excelled in the gauntlet drill, solidifying his status as a potential first-round WR pick.
Romello Height of Texas Tech impressed at the combine with strong test results, emerging as a Day 2 EDGE prospect.
Eli Stowers from Vanderbilt set a record with a 45.5-inch vertical jump, positioning him as a top TE draft choice.

title: Insider: Patriots probably won’t make a ‘Micah Parsons-type’ trade this offseason
author: Conor Roche
bullets: The Patriots are unlikely to trade for star players like A.J. Brown or Maxx Crosby this offseason, according to reports.
ESPN's Mike Reiss suggests the Patriots won't expend significant draft capital for a 'Micah Parsons-type' trade.
Patriots coach Mike Vrabel emphasizes building through the draft, with New England holding 11 picks in the 2026 NFL Draft.

title: Largest energy storage facility statewide gets approved for construction in Everett
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jupiter Power gains approval for Massachusetts' largest battery energy storage facility on former ExxonMobil site in Everett.
The facility will include 816 enclosures with integrated systems, two substations, and connect to Eversource's Mystic Substation.
This project is part of Everett's broader 'Docklands' development, featuring significant residential, office, and retail spaces.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Connor Storrie hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Connor Storrie hosted the 13th episode of SNL's 51st season on February 28, with Mumford & Sons as musical guest.
Hudson Williams joined Storrie on SNL, appearing in a sketch and introducing Mumford & Sons as the musical guest.
US Olympic hockey team members, including Hilary Knight, appeared during the SNL episode, sharing comedic moments on stage.

title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s loss against the Red Bulls
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution lost 1-0 to the New York Red Bulls at Sports Illustrated Stadium.
Revolution struggled against New York's pressing, resulting in only 27% possession in the first half.
A missed second-half opportunity highlighted Revolution's ongoing difficulties in converting chances into goals.
